assertions.text: V1 and V2 were traveling northbound on a four-lane, physically divided bidirectional roadway (two lanes northbound, two lanes southbound).
Both vehicles were negotiating a right-hand curve in the roadway with a slight incline.
V1 was traveling in the leftmost northbound lane (lane one).
V2 was traveling in the adjacent right northbound lane (lane two) to the right of V1.
V1 began to move from lane one into lane two while V2 was attempting to pass V1.
V2 departed its lane to the right (left relative to V1) prior to impact.
V2's frontal plane impacted V1's rear plane (V2 struck the rear of V1 with its front).
There was a single distinct impact between V2 and V1 described (one collision event).
V2 came to final rest off the right side of the roadway.
There was a small fire in the engine compartment of V2 after the collision.
V1 continued northbound after the impact and was not located (V1 departed the scene/was not found).
The collision produced enough damage/fire to cause a small engine compartment fire in V2.

verdicts.evidence: Roadway geometry (four-lane, physically divided, directions) is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry and cannot be inferred from the provided channels; this is unverifiable from available sensors.

Curvature and incline of the roadway are not directly measured by the device-frame sensors without map/GPS heading/elevation; steering shows a peak of [35.0](T2) degrees at [t=0.0](T2) but that alone does not prove a right-hand curve with slight incline, so the assertion is unverifiable.
Lane position (leftmost northbound lane) is not recorded in telemetry; GPS lane-level position is absent, so this is unverifiable.
Lane assignment (V2 in lane two to right of V1) cannot be determined from the available channels, so this is unverifiable.

The sequence claiming V1 began to move into V2's lane while V2 attempted to pass cannot be established from V2-only telemetry; steering shows a peak of [35.0](T2) degrees at [t=0.0](T2) and a brake transition at [t=-0.5](T2), but these do not prove the described lane change by V1, so unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a lateral crash-pulse with cumulative lateral delta-V peaking at [11.0 km/h](T1) (value [ -11.0](T2) km/h at [t=0.09 s](T1)), indicating V2 experienced a lateral motion consistent with departing its lane to the right prior to impact.

EDR summary labels the event as a ["Frontal / Rear Crash (Record 1)"](T1) and the cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaches [ -81.0 km/h](T1) at [t=185 ms](T1), with negative sign indicating a deceleration consistent with V2's front impacting another vehicle's rear.

The crash-pulse shows a single dominant longitudinal delta-V peak of [81.0 km/h](T3) at [t=0.16 s](T2) and the detect_events output reports one impact delta-V event for longitudinal and one for lateral, consistent with a single distinct collision event.
Final rest position off the right side of the roadway is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry; GPS final position is not provided, so this is unverifiable.
Fire in the engine compartment is not measured by vehicle sensors in the available data; no temperature or fire-detection channel is present, so this is unverifiable.
Whether V1 continued northbound after impact and was not located cannot be determined from V2-only telemetry; therefore unverifiable.

The collision produced a high longitudinal delta-V of [ -81.0 km/h](T1) recorded in the EDR summary and detect_events, indicating severe impact energy, which supports that the crash could produce engine damage sufficient to cause a small engine fire.

result.rationale: Device-only telemetry (V2) provides strong evidence for impact direction and a single severe collision (delta-V longitudinal [ -81.0 km/h](T1) and lateral [ -11.0 km/h](T1)), but lane-level positions, final rest location, and fire presence are not recorded; several narrative points are therefore unverifiable.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: delta-V peak of   at   and the detect_events output reports one impact delta-V event for longitudinal and one for lateral, consist

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
